architecture: Irregular infiltrative glands embedded in abundant desmoplastic stroma.
Tubular, tubulopapillary or anastomosing glandular structures.
cytology: Mitoses are common and nuclei are vesicular with small nucleoli.

mucin_features: Intraluminal or intracytoplasmic mucin strongly supports iCCA.
PAS-D and Alcian blue highlight mucin components.

immunohistochemistry: iCCA: CK19+, MOC-31+, CK7+, mucin+, HepPar-1−.
HCC: HepPar-1+, GPC3+, CD34 sinusoidal, CK19−.

mixed_type_cholangiocarcinoma.definition: Combined hepatocellular–cholangiocarcinoma (cHCC-CCA) shows both hepatocytic and cholangiocytic elements.
diagnostic_logic: Identify areas of classic HCC morphology and classic iCCA morphology.
Dual immunophenotype possible: HepPar-1+/CK19− adjacent to HepPar-1−/CK19+.
Mixed or stem cell–like types may show overlapping features.
